findings.title: Recommended backup includes Passwords app preferences
findings.suggested_fix: Remove com.apple.Passwords from the recommended apple_apps domain list, or move credential-adjacent Apple app domains behind a custom-only opt-in with an explicit privacy warning. Add a regression that recommended macOS preference backup does not export com.apple.Passwords.plist.

residual_risks: Several intentionally supported report categories can still place privacy-sensitive local metadata into a dotfiles repo, including host names, hardware port details, Time Machine destination details, printer URIs, and login/background-item entries. This appears aligned with the stated feature intent, but users should understand the generated repo may contain machine-identifying data before making it public.
The review did not inspect live exported plist contents because the request was scoped to read-only checkout review; exact secret/PII presence may vary by macOS version and local user state.
testing_gaps: No regression asserts that recommended macOS preferences exclude credential-adjacent domains such as com.apple.Passwords.
No fixture validates that generated macOS reports avoid credentials or tokens embedded in service URLs, backup destinations, or printer configuration output.
